title_zh: 多频段手机天线的仿真研究与设计
title_en: Simulation Research and Design of Multiband Mobile Phone Antenna
author: 王艺微
college: 物理与电子信息工程学院
keywords_en: mobile phone antenna; PIFA; multiband; HFSS; n78
abstract_zh: 随着5G Sub-6 GHz、无线局域网和智能终端业务的持续发展，手机天线需要在有限净空和复杂整机环境中同时覆盖多个工作频段。针对“多频段手机天线”的设计要求，本文以平面倒F天线（PIFA）为基础，在Ansys HFSS中建立包含手机地板、馈电点、短路点、主PIFA辐射枝节和右侧耦合寄生枝节的手机天线模型。论文把WLAN 2.4 GHz、5G n78和WLAN 5.8 GHz作为统一设计目标，而不是只围绕单一中心频点展开，重点分析不同电流路径、寄生耦合和馈电参数对三段阻抗匹配的影响。

本文第一章论述多频段手机天线的研究背景、国内外研究现状和研究意义，明确论文围绕WLAN 2.4 GHz、5G n78与WLAN 5.8 GHz三个频段展开；第二章论述手机天线基本参数、PIFA结构原理、多频段实现技术以及HFSS仿真流程；第三章论述多频段手机PIFA天线的结构建模、参数设置和谐振机理，说明主辐射枝节、手机地板和寄生枝节对三段频率的作用；第四章结合重新仿真的HFSS宽频数据，对最终模型的S11、VSWR、输入阻抗、Smith圆图、参数组对比和n78局部复核结果进行分析。仿真结果表明，HFSSDesign_phone_multiband_enhanced模型在2.4~2.5 GHz、3.3~3.8 GHz和5.725~5.85 GHz三个频段内的最差S11分别为-30.56 dB、-10.37 dB和-16.41 dB，均满足S11小于-10 dB的匹配判据；第五章总结全文工作，并对后续结构优化、效率分析和实物测试方向进行展望。
abstract_en: With the development of 5G Sub-6 GHz systems, wireless local area networks, and smart terminal services, mobile phone antennas are required to cover multiple operating bands within limited clearance and a complex handset environment. For the topic of a multiband mobile phone antenna, this thesis develops a planar inverted-F antenna (PIFA) model in Ansys HFSS by combining the handset ground, feed point, shorting point, main PIFA radiating branch, and a right-side coupled parasitic branch. WLAN 2.4 GHz, 5G n78, and WLAN 5.8 GHz are treated as unified design targets rather than as isolated center frequencies, and the influence of current paths, parasitic coupling, and feed parameters on three-band impedance matching is analyzed.

Chapter 1 discusses the research background, current research status, and significance of multiband mobile phone antennas, clarifying that this thesis focuses on WLAN 2.4 GHz, 5G n78, and WLAN 5.8 GHz bands. Chapter 2 introduces the basic parameters of mobile phone antennas, the operating principle of PIFA structures, multiband implementation methods, and the HFSS simulation procedure. Chapter 3 presents the structural modeling, parameter setting, and resonance mechanism of the proposed multiband handset PIFA antenna, explaining the roles of the main radiating branch, handset ground, and parasitic branch in the three target bands. Chapter 4 analyzes the final model through the newly simulated wideband HFSS data, including S11, VSWR, input impedance, Smith-chart results, parameter-group comparison, and local n78 verification. The selected HFSSDesign_phone_multiband_enhanced model achieves worst-case S11 values of -30.56 dB, -10.37 dB, and -16.41 dB in the 2.4-2.5 GHz, 3.3-3.8 GHz, and 5.725-5.85 GHz bands, respectively, satisfying the S11 less than -10 dB criterion in all three bands. Chapter 5 summarizes the whole work and discusses future improvements in structural optimization, efficiency analysis, and prototype measurement.
